notes.summary: A polygon is a closed figure with straight sides. Polygons have properties such as number of sides, angles, and diagonals. The sum of interior angles in any polygon can be calculated using the formula (n-2)*180 degrees, where n is the number of sides.
notes.importance: Understanding polygon properties helps in identifying and calculating various geometric figures.
quick_facts: A polygon has straight sides.
The sum of interior angles in a polygon is (n-2)*180 degrees.
Each exterior angle of a polygon is equal to 360/n degrees.
A regular polygon has equal interior angles.

notes.summary: There are two main types of polygons: regular and irregular. Regular polygons have all sides and angles equal, while irregular polygons do not.
notes.importance: Identifying the type of polygon helps in understanding its properties and characteristics.
quick_facts: Regular polygons have all sides and angles equal.
Irregular polygons do not have all sides and angles equal.
A regular hexagon has six equal sides and interior angles.
An irregular pentagon has five unequal sides and interior angles.

notes.summary: A tetrahedron is a three-dimensional shape with four triangular faces. It has six edges and four vertices.
notes.importance: Understanding the properties of a tetrahedron helps in identifying its characteristics and uses in geometry.
quick_facts: A tetrahedron has four triangular faces.
It has six edges and four vertices.
The sum of interior angles in any tetrahedron is 720 degrees.
Each face of a tetrahedron is an equilateral triangle.

notes.summary: A sphere is a three-dimensional shape that is perfectly round and has no corners or edges. It has only one surface.
notes.importance: Understanding the properties of a sphere helps in identifying its characteristics and uses in geometry.
quick_facts: A sphere has no corners or edges.
It has only one surface.
The sum of interior angles in any polygon inscribed within a sphere is 360 degrees.
Each cross-section of a sphere is a circle.

notes.summary: The Chessboard Grains Puzzle demonstrates the power of exponential growth. It shows how a small amount can grow into an enormous quantity in just a few steps.
notes.importance: Understanding the puzzle helps in visualizing and calculating large numbers and their properties.
quick_facts: The first square has one grain of rice.
Each subsequent square has twice as many grains as the previous square.
By the 64th square, the total number of grains is 18 quintillion.
This number exceeds global rice production by over 1,000 times.
